raw_response: \boxed{D} The evidence consistently indicates that apical periodontitis primarily originates from sequelae of pulpal diseases, as evidenced by processes involving necrotic pulp and subsequent microbial invasion into periapical tissues. Additionally, it highlights potential contributing factors such as wedging of objects, high points in restorations, and other forms of traumatic injuries, making all listed causes plausible contributors to apical periodontitis. Thus, option D encompassing all these possibilities aligns best with the presented information.
